competitors.name: Wiz
competitors.watchFor: Cloud-only positioning, agentless messaging, CNAPP consolidation pitches

competitors.name: Qualys
competitors.watchFor: TruRisk score comparisons, VM pricing pressure, platform bundling offers

competitors.name: CrowdStrike
competitors.watchFor: Spotlight bundling with Falcon, endpoint-to-exposure upsell, identity protection push

competitors.name: Rapid7
competitors.watchFor: VM displacement plays, MDR bundling, cloud security expansion

competitors.name: Palo Alto Networks
competitors.watchFor: CNAPP consolidation, ASM + VM bundling, platform pricing pressure

news.headline: Google completes $32B acquisition of Wiz
news.summary: Google officially completed its acquisition of Wiz for $32 billion in cash — the largest acquisition in Google's history. Wiz joins Google Cloud but retains its brand and its commitment to supporting all major cloud environments including AWS and Azure.
news.tenableImpact: Wiz is now a Google product. For customers who run multi-cloud or are not Google-first, this creates a legitimate concern about long-term vendor neutrality. Wiz has publicly committed to supporting all clouds, but that message will need repeating. The acquisition also deepens Wiz's financial resources and engineering capacity significantly.
news.responseGuidance: Acknowledge the acquisition as a major development. Then reframe: Tenable is the only independent exposure management platform — not owned by a cloud provider with a vested interest in that cloud's adoption. For AWS- or Azure-heavy customers, this is a real differentiator. Ask: 'Now that Wiz is a Google product, how does your leadership feel about a Google-owned tool having visibility into your AWS and Azure environments?'

news.headline: Wiz launches AI-Application Protection Platform (AI-APP) and Security Agents at RSA 2026
news.summary: At RSA 2026, Wiz announced its AI-APP platform providing end-to-end security for AI applications, plus three agentic security agents: Red Agent (offensive/attack simulation), Blue Agent (investigation, now GA), and Green Agent (remediation). Also launched Wiz Workflows for security process automation.
news.tenableImpact: Wiz is directly entering the agentic AI security space — their clearest move yet to compete with Hexa AI. Blue Agent became GA on March 30. However, Wiz's agents operate within the Wiz Security Graph, covering cloud-native environments. They have no equivalent cross-surface capability across OT, identity, and AI simultaneously.
news.responseGuidance: Acknowledge Blue Agent is a genuine product — don't dismiss it. Then pivot: 'Wiz's agents are impressive within their cloud context. Hexa AI orchestrates across IT, cloud, OT, identity, and AI in a single workflow — and it runs on the Exposure Data Fabric, not just cloud telemetry. That broader context is the difference between a cloud remediation tool and a programme-wide automation engine.'

news.headline: Wiz expands Security Graph to Databricks, AWS AgentCore, Azure Copilot Studio, Salesforce Agentforce at Google Cloud Next
news.summary: At Google Cloud Next '26, Wiz announced expansion of its Security Graph to cover Databricks and major AI agent-building platforms (AWS AgentCore, Gemini Enterprise, Azure Copilot Studio, Salesforce Agentforce). Also announced Wiz Defend integration with Google SecOps and Mandiant, plus new AI-native development lifecycle security features including inline AI code scanning and agent-based remediation.
news.tenableImpact: Wiz is rapidly expanding its AI coverage footprint. The Databricks integration and AI agent studio coverage are competitive with Tenable AI Exposure's direction. However, all of these integrations feed into the Wiz Security Graph — which is still Google-cloud-centric at its core. The Google SecOps integration reinforces the Google ecosystem lock-in concern.
news.responseGuidance: Use the Google ecosystem angle: Wiz is deepening its integration with Google's stack at every turn. For customers not going all-in on Google Cloud, that creates dependency risk. Tenable integrates with all clouds and all SIEMs without a stake in which cloud wins.

news.headline: Qualys launches Agent Val — agentic AI for exploit validation inside ETM
news.summary: Qualys introduced Agent Val into its Enterprise TruRisk Management platform at RSA 2026. The new agentic AI layer uses TruConfirm to safely validate exploitability of high-risk exposures directly in production environments, incorporating business context and asset criticality. Part of Qualys's broader push toward an agentic AI-native Risk Operations Center (ROC).
news.tenableImpact: Agent Val is Qualys's direct answer to Hexa AI, announced the same week at RSA. It automates one specific workflow — exploit validation — within the ETM platform. Tenable Hexa AI orchestrates across all five surfaces and multiple workflow types simultaneously. Agent Val is narrower but concrete and GA.
news.responseGuidance: Acknowledge Agent Val is a genuine product capability and a real step forward for Qualys. Then differentiate on scope: 'Agent Val validates exploitability within Qualys ETM. Hexa AI orchestrates remediation across IT, cloud, OT, identity, and AI — it's not one workflow in one product, it's programme-wide automation. Also worth noting: Hexa AI lets you configure human-in-the-loop approval at every stage. Ask how much control your team needs before an agent takes autonomous action.'

news.headline: Qualys building agentic AI-native Risk Operations Center (ROC) with ISPM and AI marketplace
news.summary: Qualys is positioning its ETM platform toward an agentic AI-native Risk Operations Center model — not just detecting vulnerabilities but autonomously remediating them. Added Identity Security Posture Management (ISPM) to the ETM platform and launched an AI marketplace for deploying specialised autonomous agents for patch management and exploit validation.
news.tenableImpact: Qualys is directionally moving toward Tenable One's positioning — unified risk management with autonomous remediation. The ISPM addition is notable because identity was previously a clear Tenable advantage. Their AI marketplace approach is modular rather than platform-native, which creates integration complexity.
news.responseGuidance: The ISPM addition warrants attention for customers where Identity Exposure is part of the value story. Tenable Identity Exposure is purpose-built with deep AD expertise and 20+ years of identity security research behind it — ISPM is a new addition to Qualys ETM, not a core capability. On the ROC/agentic direction: Qualys is building towards what Tenable One already is today.

news.headline: CrowdStrike positions Falcon endpoint as the AI security epicenter at RSA 2026
news.summary: At RSA 2026, CrowdStrike announced new Falcon platform capabilities extending AI agent discovery, shadow AI governance, and runtime protection from the endpoint across SaaS, browser, and cloud environments. Core message: the endpoint is the point of AI execution, and therefore the enforcement point for AI security.
news.tenableImpact: CrowdStrike's 'endpoint as epicenter' framing is clever but reveals its limitation — it's still endpoint-anchored. AI agents executing on unmanaged cloud infrastructure, OT devices, or identity systems without an endpoint agent are outside Falcon's view. Tenable covers all five surfaces including those where no Falcon agent is deployed.
news.responseGuidance: Validate the endpoint insight: 'CrowdStrike is right that AI agents execute on endpoints. But they also execute on cloud functions, identity systems, and OT infrastructure without an endpoint agent. Ask your customer: of all the places AI is running in your environment, what percentage have a Falcon sensor? Tenable covers the rest.'

news.headline: CrowdStrike launches Falcon Data Security and Agentic MDR at RSA 2026
news.summary: CrowdStrike launched Falcon Data Security — covering data theft prevention across endpoints, SaaS, cloud, browsers, and AI workflows using adversary intelligence. Also unveiled Agentic MDR (Managed Detection and Response), using AI agents to automate investigation and response workflows within Falcon Complete. Cited 89% YoY increase in AI-enabled adversary operations and 29-minute average eCrime breakout time.
news.tenableImpact: Falcon Data Security extends CrowdStrike further into DSPM territory and is a more credible data security play than before. Agentic MDR is their answer to the automated SOC trend. Neither directly competes with Tenable's exposure management positioning — these are detection and response capabilities, not proactive exposure reduction.
news.responseGuidance: Falcon Data Security and Agentic MDR are detection and response products. Tenable is proactive exposure management — finding and closing the paths before attackers use them. These can coexist: 'Tenable tells you where you're exposed. CrowdStrike tells you when you've been hit. Both matter, but if you only have budget for one investment, the question is whether you want to stop breaches before they start or respond to them faster.'

news.headline: Palo Alto Networks announces Prisma AIRS 3.0 and agentic AI security at RSA 2026
news.summary: Palo Alto introduced Prisma AIRS 3.0 — a platform to secure the full agentic AI lifecycle — along with Prisma Browser for Business and Next-Generation Trust Security for post-quantum readiness. Continued push toward AI security posture management across agent deployment pipelines.
news.tenableImpact: Prisma AIRS competes with Tenable AI Exposure on the AI security posture management front. Palo Alto's approach is Prisma-platform-centric — it has deep cloud integration but limited coverage of on-premises, OT, or identity environments. The post-quantum readiness angle is forward-looking but not yet a mainstream customer concern.
news.responseGuidance: For customers evaluating Prisma AIRS vs Tenable AI Exposure: Tenable's AI Aware capability detects AI software and CVEs on any asset — not just cloud workloads. AI Exposure covers governance and shadow AI regardless of where it's running. Palo Alto's coverage starts at the cloud boundary.

news.title: Tenable responds to Agent Val with Hexa AI announcement at RSA 2026
news.summary: On the same day as RSA Conference 2026, Tenable announced Tenable Hexa AI — the agentic AI engine of the Tenable One platform. Hexa AI is Tenable's direct competitive response to Qualys Agent Val. It orchestrates complex multi-step security workflows across IT, cloud, identity, OT, and AI environments simultaneously, powered by the Tenable Exposure Data Fabric. Currently in private preview for select Tenable One customers, with GA expected later in 2026.
news.tenableImpact: This is a strong competitive moment for Tenable. Hexa AI directly addresses the agentic AI narrative that Qualys is building with Agent Val, and does so with a broader architectural scope. The key advantage to communicate: Hexa AI orchestrates across all five surfaces simultaneously — Agent Val automates one workflow (exploit validation) within one platform (Qualys ETM). The Exposure Data Fabric gives Hexa AI contextual awareness that Agent Val cannot replicate across non-Qualys data sources. The timing of the RSA announcement is also strategically significant — Tenable is matching Qualys's agentic AI narrative in real time at the industry's most visible security conference.
news.howToRespond: Use this proactively in any account where Agent Val has come up: 'You may have seen Qualys announce Agent Val at RSA — it's a strong capability within their platform. What you may not have seen yet is that Tenable announced Hexa AI on the same day. Hexa AI is the agentic engine inside Tenable One that orchestrates workflows across your entire attack surface — not just IT vulnerability validation. If you're evaluating agentic AI for security, it's worth seeing both side by side before making any decisions.' This is a natural reason to book a Tenable One conversation at any account that attended or followed RSA.

news.title: Qualys launches Agent Val — agentic AI for exploit validation inside ETM
news.summary: Qualys introduced Agent Val, an agentic AI workflow inside Qualys Enterprise TruRisk Management (ETM) that automates exploit validation across a closed-loop cycle: validate whether a vulnerability is actually exploitable in the live environment, select the optimal remediation path, and revalidate that the exploit path is closed after remediation. Agent Val uses TruConfirm to safely prove exploitability without production impact. Qualys positions this as the industry's first complete automated CTEM pipeline.
news.tenableImpact:
Agent Val is a direct competitive response to Tenable Hexa AI, announced today at RSA Conference 2026. Both are agentic AI systems that automate security workflows — but they take meaningfully different architectural approaches.

Qualys Agent Val focuses narrowly on exploit validation: proving whether a specific vulnerability is actually exploitable in a specific environment, then remediating it and revalidating closure. It is essentially an agentic wrapper around TruConfirm, Qualys's existing exploit validation capability. It is strong within the Qualys ETM platform but is limited to assets already in that inventory.

Tenable Hexa AI is broader in scope — it is an orchestration engine that coordinates workflows across IT, cloud, identity, OT, and AI environments simultaneously. Rather than focusing on a single workflow (validate → remediate → revalidate), Hexa AI can execute complex multi-step workflows across the entire attack surface, with both out-of-the-box agents and custom agents. It is powered by the Tenable Exposure Data Fabric, which gives it contextual awareness across all five surfaces rather than just vulnerability data.

The practical competitive distinction: Agent Val wins on depth within IT vulnerability validation. Hexa AI wins on breadth — coordinating across surfaces Agent Val cannot reach. For customers evaluating both, the question to ask is: do you need a smarter way to validate and close IT vulnerabilities, or do you need agentic orchestration across your entire attack surface? Note that Hexa AI is currently in private preview for select Tenable One customers — GA is expected later in 2026, so be prepared for customers to ask about timeline.
news.howToRespond: Acknowledge that Agent Val is a genuine capability — exploit validation is a real and meaningful workflow improvement. Then reframe the comparison: 'Agent Val is excellent at proving whether a specific IT vulnerability is exploitable and closing it — that's a deep, focused capability. Tenable Hexa AI is doing something architecturally broader: it's orchestrating workflows across IT, cloud, identity, OT, and AI simultaneously. If your goal is to automate your entire exposure management program — not just one workflow within it — Hexa AI is built for that scope. Agent Val doesn't have an answer for what happens outside the Qualys inventory.' If asked about Hexa AI availability, be transparent: it's in private preview now with GA later in 2026, and customers can contact their account team to join the preview program.

news.title: Wiz expands AI-SPM capabilities with runtime threat detection
news.summary: Wiz announced expanded AI Security Posture Management features including runtime threat detection for AI workloads. Adds visibility into model behavior and data access patterns during inference — moving closer to Tenable's AI Exposure territory.
news.howToRespond: Acknowledge Wiz is strong in cloud AI posture, but point out they still have no coverage for shadow AI (tools adopted without IT approval), non-human identity governance, or AI risk outside the cloud boundary. Tenable AI Exposure covers the full enterprise AI surface.
news.tenableImpact: Wiz moving into AI runtime detection narrows the gap with Tenable AI Exposure on the cloud side — but only on the cloud side. Tenable still owns the broader AI attack surface story including shadow AI, non-human identities, and AI risk outside cloud environments. Use this as a prompt to accelerate AI Exposure conversations before Wiz plants a flag with cloud-first customers.

news.title: Wiz reportedly offering aggressive discounts to displace CNAPP incumbents
news.summary: Multiple field reports indicate Wiz is offering 30-40% discounts to customers evaluating CNAPP replacements, particularly targeting Prisma Cloud and Tenable Cloud Security customers ahead of renewals.
news.howToRespond: Don't compete on price alone — compete on breadth. Wiz discounts to $0 if they only cover cloud. Tenable covers 5 surfaces. Ask the customer: 'What does Wiz cover for your on-prem, OT, and identity environments?' The silence answers the question.
news.tenableImpact: Aggressive Wiz discounting is a displacement threat for Tenable Cloud Security renewals, particularly at the CNAPP layer. However, it's also an opportunity — customers being courted by Wiz are actively thinking about cloud security gaps, which opens the door to the Tenable One platform story. Counter by widening the scope of the conversation beyond cloud.

news.title: Qualys launches TotalCloud 2.0 with expanded CNAPP capabilities
news.summary: Qualys announced TotalCloud 2.0 with improved CSPM, new container security features, and tighter integration with VMDR. Positions it as a unified cloud + VM platform to compete with Tenable One's cross-surface story.
news.howToRespond: TotalCloud is Qualys adding cloud capabilities on top of a VM product — it's additive, not architected as a platform. Tenable One was built as a unified exposure platform from the ground up. Key gaps Qualys still can't fill: dedicated AD security, passive OT monitoring, AI exposure, and cross-surface attack path analysis.
news.tenableImpact: TotalCloud 2.0 is Qualys's attempt to tell a platform story similar to Tenable One — but it's architecturally bolted together rather than natively built. Tenable One has a meaningful head start in cross-surface coverage (OT, identity, AI) that Qualys cannot close quickly. Low immediate threat, but worth monitoring as Qualys invests more in the platform narrative.

news.title: Qualys introduces asset-based pricing bundles targeting Tenable VM renewals
news.summary: Qualys field teams are actively pitching bundled VMDR + TruRisk packages at renewal time with price-match guarantees targeting Tenable Vulnerability Management customers, particularly in the mid-market.
news.howToRespond: If a customer brings up Qualys pricing: reframe the conversation around total value, not line-item cost. Ask them to compare the full capability set — Qualys has no AD product, no passive OT scanner, and no AI exposure tool. The Tenable One bundle often comes out ahead on total cost when you factor in the tools it replaces.
news.tenableImpact: Direct price-matching on VM renewals is the highest-risk Qualys scenario for Tenable — particularly in mid-market accounts where the buying decision is more price-sensitive. The defense is to elevate the conversation to platform value before Qualys gets a seat at the renewal table. If Qualys is already in the room, lead with the capability gaps they cannot fill.

news.title: CrowdStrike announces Falcon for OT — limited early access
news.summary: CrowdStrike announced early access for Falcon for OT, an agent-based OT security solution targeting Windows-based industrial control systems. Currently limited to Windows ICS environments and does not support passive monitoring of Purdue model layer 0-1 devices.
news.howToRespond: Falcon for OT requires an agent on Windows-based ICS systems — it cannot passively monitor PLCs, HMIs, SCADA systems, or legacy OT devices at the network level. Tenable OT Security's passive monitoring approach means zero risk of disrupting operational systems. For any customer with true OT environments, this is a significant architectural difference.
news.tenableImpact: Falcon for OT is a meaningful competitive development — CrowdStrike now has a story for industrial environments where previously they had nothing. However, the agent requirement is a fundamental architectural limitation for true OT environments. Tenable's passive monitoring approach is not a feature difference, it's a safety requirement for Layer 0-1 OT devices. Reinforce this distinction early with any OT-focused customer.

news.title: CrowdStrike bundling Falcon Spotlight 'free' with Falcon Complete renewals
news.summary: CrowdStrike is including Falcon Spotlight vulnerability management at no additional cost for customers renewing Falcon Complete (MDR) contracts, framing it as an alternative to standalone VM tools.
news.howToRespond: Spotlight being 'free' is only free if you already have complete Falcon coverage across all assets — which most enterprises don't. It sees only managed endpoints with agents. Remind the customer: unmanaged devices, network gear, OT assets, cloud infrastructure without agents, and their AD environment are all invisible to Spotlight. Free coverage of 60% of your environment still leaves 40% blind.
news.tenableImpact: Spotlight bundled 'free' with Falcon Complete is the most common CrowdStrike displacement tactic Tenable faces in enterprise accounts. It creates a perception problem — customers feel they're already paying for VM. The response is to shift the conversation from price to coverage: Spotlight's agent dependency creates systematic blind spots that represent real, unquantified risk.

news.title: Rapid7 launches Exposure Command — unified exposure management platform
news.summary: Rapid7 announced Exposure Command, a new platform layer combining InsightVM, surface command, and threat intelligence into a unified exposure management view. Direct positioning against Tenable One's Cyber Exposure Score.
news.howToRespond: Rapid7's platform story is newer and narrower — Exposure Command is primarily IT vulnerability management with some ASM. It has no dedicated OT product, no purpose-built AD/identity security, and no AI exposure capability. Tenable One covers all 5 surfaces with purpose-built products. Also worth noting: Tenable was named a Leader in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Exposure Assessment Platforms — Rapid7 was not.
news.tenableImpact: Rapid7 Exposure Command is a direct competitive response to Tenable One's platform positioning. It signals that the market is validating Tenable's exposure management narrative — but Rapid7's version is narrower and newer. Use the Gartner MQ leadership position as a third-party proof point when customers ask about Rapid7's platform story.

news.title: Palo Alto expands Cortex Xpanse with internal attack surface capabilities
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks announced Xpanse Active ASM now includes some internal network discovery features, attempting to bridge external and internal attack surface coverage. Competes more directly with Tenable's ASM + VM combination.
news.howToRespond: Xpanse is an external attack surface tool adding some internal visibility — it's still architecturally built outward-in. Tenable ASM feeds natively into Tenable VM so discovered external assets are automatically scanned internally. More importantly, Palo Alto still has no OT security product, no dedicated AD security tool, and Prisma Cloud doesn't integrate with their ASM in a unified exposure score the way Tenable One does.
news.tenableImpact: Palo Alto expanding Xpanse internally creates more overlap with Tenable's ASM + VM combination. The risk is in accounts where Palo Alto already has a strong firewall and Prisma Cloud footprint — they can position Xpanse as a natural extension. Counter by demonstrating the native ASM-to-VM workflow that Tenable offers and the broader surface coverage that Palo Alto cannot match natively.
